name: Olmestig 2025 ETLAS-2 Blood Pressure and CSVD Vascular Extract
population_or_context: Older patients with cerebral small vessel disease and previous stroke or transient ischemic attack.
methods: ETLAS-2 randomized placebo-controlled double-blind phase II trial of tadalafil 20 mg daily for 3 months; secondary outcomes included blood pressure, cognition, imaging markers, mental well-being, and adverse events.
population_profile: Older CSVD patients, mean age 68, with prior stroke/TIA; indirect for healthy men age 30 to 50.
indication_context: Experimental cerebral small vessel disease trial.
study_design: Randomized placebo-controlled double-blind parallel phase II trial.
safety_findings: Adverse events occurred in 76% with tadalafil versus 36% with placebo, and adherence was lower with tadalafil.
directness_to_target_population: Low for healthy men age 30 to 50 because participants were older with CSVD and prior stroke/TIA.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Feasibility trial, older cerebrovascular disease population, higher dose than common 5 mg daily ED/BPH use, and secondary BP endpoint.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is CSVD population, dose, adherence, and endpoint context.
